idea-plugin.name: Python Role Lens
idea-plugin.description:
Adds IDE awareness for the Role / delegation pattern in Python, where a class subclasses Role[SomeTaker] and forwards attribute access to a wrapped instance through __getattr__.

The members of the taker class — together with everything it inherits, and any nested roles — become available on the role instance for code completion, type inference, and Go to Declaration (Ctrl/Cmd+Click), without using inheritance.

Getting started

Install the plugin and restart — there is nothing to configure. It activates automatically for any class that takes on a role by subclassing a Role[Taker] base that delegates through __getattr__. 

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from krrood.patterns.role import Role

@dataclass
class Person:
    name: str
    age: int
    def greet(self) -> str:
        return f"Hi, I'm {self.name}"

@dataclass
class Teacher(Role[Person]):                    # the taker type is the generic argument
    courses: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

teacher = Teacher(role_taker=Person("Ahmed", 20))
teacher.name       # completes, inferred as str
teacher.greet()    # completes; Ctrl/Cmd-Click jumps to Person.greet
teacher.courses    # the role's own field

What you get

  • Code completion after . on a role instance — the taker's attributes and methods appear.
  • Type inference — a delegated attribute is typed from its real declaration.
  • Go to Declaration (Ctrl/Cmd+Click), Find Usages, and Rename all resolve to the real member in the taker class.

It also handles transitive roles (a subclass of a role), inherited taker members (the taker's own base classes), and nested roles (a taker that is itself a role).

Requirements

Works out of the box with krrood's Role, and with any Role[...] base that delegates via __getattr__. The role taker is read from the Role[...] generic argument.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.2.0 — The role taker is now resolved solely from the Role[Taker] generic argument, matching krrood's move to an inherited keyword-only role_taker field (the role_taker_field() declaration was removed). Roles must declare Role[Taker] for their taker's members to be injected.
0.1.0 — Initial release. Completion, type inference, and Go to Declaration for the Role / delegation pattern: the taker's members (plus everything it inherits and any nested roles) are injected onto a role declared with Role[Taker]. A Role[T] parameterised by a bounded TypeVar resolves to its bound.
